facts_disclosed: Works in audit at PwC Manchester

turns.content: im genuinely losing my mind. just got home and its half midnight and I have to be back in for 7am. someone tell me why I chose this career path loool
That's a brutal schedule. What kind of work has you pulling those hours?
audit. I work at PwC in Manchester, junior level. and right now it's busy season which basically means your life is not your own for like 3 months straight
Ah, Big 4 busy season — that's legendary for a reason. January through March, right? Or is your team on a different cycle?

nah it depends on the client year-ends but yeah we're deep in it right now. some of our clients have December year-ends so the prep starts November and then January to March is just pure chaos. like today I was in the office from 7 doing walkthroughs and didnt leave till half 11
That's almost a 17-hour day. How are you holding up physically? That pace can wear you down fast.

bare tired tbh. like my body is running on caffeine and willpower at this point. and the worst part is the seniors just accept it as normal?? like they'll casually say 'oh this is nothing wait till Jan' and I'm sat there thinking I might actually collapse
The normalization of it is the worst part. When everyone around you treats 17-hour days as just "how it is," it's hard to even feel like you can complain. Are you at least getting weekends off?

weekends are lighter but not off. like Saturday I might only do 4-5 hours. Sunday I try to keep free but theres always emails. and I live in Manchester so at least I dont have a mad commute, the office is only like 20 mins from mine

A short commute is a lifesaver when you're doing those hours. At least you're not adding an hour each way on top of everything. Is there an end date you're working toward, or does busy season just taper off gradually?
it tapers tbh. like March things start calming down and by April it's somewhat normal hours again. but normal at PwC is still like 9 to 7 most days loool. it's not a 9 to 5 ever
Right, so "normal" is still 10-hour days — busy season just makes it 15+. That's the Big 4 trade-off. Do you feel like it's worth it for your career, or are you questioning that right now?

honestly right now at midnight on a Tuesday I'm questioning everything loool. but like objectively yeah it looks good on the CV init. everyone says the first 2-3 years are the worst and then you either get promoted or you leave for something better. so I'm just trying to survive basically

That's the classic Big 4 playbook — grind now, cash in later. And it does work for a lot of people. The PwC name carries serious weight whether you stay or go. But surviving is the key word. What are you doing to keep yourself sane during the busy period?

honestly not enough. I barely cook, I havent been to the gym in weeks, I see my family maybe once a week if that. basically I work, I eat whatever I can find, I sleep for 5 hours, repeat. its giving robot energy

That's not sustainable even for a few months. A couple of small things that might help: batch-cook something easy on Sunday when you have that lighter day, even if it's just rice and chicken or something quick. And protect your sleep — even getting 6 hours instead of 5 makes a measurable difference in how your brain works. You're doing complex audit work on barely any sleep, and that compounds over weeks.

the cooking thing is real. I keep saying I'll meal prep on Sunday and then Sunday comes and I just lie on the sofa staring at the ceiling. maybe I need to actually commit to it this week though because the takeaway spending is getting out of hand

If a full meal prep feels like too much on Sunday, even just making a big pot of something — stew, curry, whatever you like — counts. One pot, minimal effort, portions for the week. The takeaway costs add up fast, especially over a 3-month busy season. Set a small goal: one batch cook this Sunday. That's it. Don't try to do five containers of perfectly portioned meals.
yeah thats actually doable. one pot. I can manage that wallah. anyway I need to actually go to sleep now cos that alarm is going off in 5 hours and I will cry. thanks for letting me vent tho
Go sleep! And good luck with the rest of busy season. One pot on Sunday — that's the mission. Hope tomorrow's a slightly shorter day.
